preamble: Assibilation of palatalized voiced dental stops occurs when a voiced dental stop [d] undergoes palatalization and then evolves into a sibilant affricate or fricative. This process typically proceeds through an intermediate stage of a voiced sibilant affricate like [dz] before simplifying to a voiced fricative [z].

examples.note: Development of Latin [dj] sequence in 'hordeum' > Italian 'orzo' [dz] or 'radiu' > Old French 'rais' [z].
The Second Palatalization results in Russian 'vrazi' [vrazʲi] (enemies) from earlier [vradzʲi], showing the transition through a voiced affricate [dz] which later de-affricated to [z].

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
